hypothesis: If a Transformer with patch-based spectral embeddings is pretrained on large-scale simulated IR spectra and fine-tuned on experimental pure-compound spectra while jointly optimizing a reconstruction alignment loss, then it will accurately identify the molecular components of binary and ternary IR mixture spectra across concentration ratios (including experimental ATR-IR) because the auxiliary alignment disentangles pure-component features and the pretraining captures transferable vibrational patterns.
